metadata.title: The defense primes — Lockheed Martin, RTX/Raytheon, Leonardo S.p.A. vs Leonardo DRS, the FCPA/bribery record, the ELSAG surveillance arm, and the QRL/XMSS post-quantum patent thread
metadata.status: ownership, the FCPA/defective-pricing settlements, the FOCI proxy structure, and the QRL/XMSS patent (assignee Lockheed Martin) are FACT (DOJ/SEC/SEC filings/Google Patents bibliographic data); composition guard; adjudicated vs alleged graded
metadata.frame: Builds out the Defense_Primes node into the named majors and a specific tech thread. Four strands: (1) the US PRIMES (Lockheed Martin, RTX/Raytheon) and their FCPA/defective-pricing record; (2) the LEONARDO structure — Leonardo S.p.A. (Italian, ~30% state-owned via MEF) vs its US-listed subsidiary Leonardo DRS under a DoD FOCI proxy agreement — plus the Finmeccanica-era AgustaWestland bribery history and the ELSAG ALPR surveillance arm; (3) the POST-QUANTUM patent thread — a real patent (US20240048369A1) couples XMSS signing with a QRL blockchain, linking the corpus's QRL node to defense PQC interest (CNSA 2.0), with the specific LMT-assignee claim flagged unverified; (4) the FOCI mechanism as a model for foreign-owned defense suppliers. Ties to Defense_Primes, Boeing, geopolitics-defense-industrial-base, QRL + macro-crqc (PQC), spec-surveillance-cyber-threat-layer (ELSAG ALPR), Italy/MEF.

the_us_primes: Lockheed Martin is the largest US defense prime (F-35, missiles/PAC-3, space, Sikorsky) — the dominant single contractor by DoD obligations. RTX (the renamed United Technologies/Raytheon parent) holds Raytheon, Pratt & Whitney, and Collins. Both are pillars of the defense-industrial base alongside Boeing (a node), Northrop Grumman, and General Dynamics. grade: fact.
the_raytheon_fcpa: RTX's Raytheon paid OVER $950M (DOJ, Oct 2024) + ~$124M (SEC) to resolve THREE schemes: (1) FOREIGN BRIBERY — ~$2M in sham subcontracts + $30M+ to a Qatari agent (a relative of the Qatari Emir, no defense background) to win Qatari military contracts (2011-2017, FCPA); (2) DEFECTIVE PRICING — defrauding the DoD on Patriot-missile and radar contracts (admitted); (3) EXPORT-CONTROL violations. ~$230.4M criminal penalty + ~$36M forfeiture; a whistleblower-aided case. grade: fact (adjudicated/admitted).
the_leonardo_structure: Leonardo S.p.A. (formerly Finmeccanica) is Italy's defense-aerospace champion, ~30% owned by the Italian state via the Ministry of Economy and Finance (MEF) — a state-owned-champion (cf. SOEs). Its US business, Leonardo DRS, is a US-listed subsidiary that must operate under a DoD FOCI (Foreign Ownership, Control or Influence) PROXY AGREEMENT: cleared US-citizen proxies exercise the voting rights so the Italian parent cannot direct classified US programs. This FOCI proxy is the standard US mechanism for foreign-owned defense suppliers — a worked example of how the state firewalls allied-but-foreign ownership. grade: fact.
the_finmeccanica_bribery: The historical record: the Finmeccanica-era AgustaWestland VVIP-helicopter scandal (India, ~EUR560M deal) saw ex-chairman Giuseppe Orsi and AgustaWestland chief Bruno Spagnolo prosecuted for bribery in Italy and India (convictions/acquittals varied across appeals; India pursued middlemen) — and 'war profiteer' campaigning by anti-arms groups. The pattern (state-champion arms exporter + intermediary bribery in import markets) rhymes with the Raytheon-Qatar case. grade: fact (the prosecutions); outcomes varied by jurisdiction/appeal — graded carefully, not 'all convicted.'
the_elsag_surveillance: Leonardo's ELSAG arm makes automatic license-plate readers (ALPR) deployed by US law enforcement — a Leonardo division inside the PRIVATE-SURVEILLANCE complex (cf. Clearview/Palantir/Flock-style mass plate capture; consumer-rights/privacy concerns about pervasive vehicle tracking). So one entity spans state-owned arms manufacture AND domestic mass-surveillance hardware. grade: fact (ELSAG ALPR products); the mass-surveillance characterization is graded interpretation.
the_qrl_pqc_patent: CONFIRMED via the patent's bibliographic data: US20240048369A1 ('Quantum resistant ledger for secure communications', application US18/358,378, filed 2023-07-25, published 2024-02-08, status PENDING) is ASSIGNED TO LOCKHEED MARTIN CORPORATION (assigned 2023-08-07; inventors Ambrose Kam and Alexander Richard Vesey; classification H04L9/0852 Quantum cryptography; also filed GB/CA/AU). The method applies a Quantum Resistant Ledger (QRL) encryption scheme to data by security level and communicates the encrypted data between network components. This DIRECTLY links the corpus's QRL node (spec-blockchain-ecosystem) to a top US defense prime's secure-comms IP — a defense-industrial-base endorsement of the QRL/XMSS post-quantum approach (consistent with CNSA 2.0's mandate to migrate to PQC; hash-based XMSS/LMS per NIST SP 800-208). NOTE: a pending patent application is a documented filing, NOT proof of deployment in fielded systems. grade: assignee + filing FACT (primary-source bibliographic); operational use NOT claimed.
the_honest_reading: The defense primes are concentrated, state-entangled, and recidivist on foreign-bribery/defective-pricing (Raytheon $950M; Finmeccanica/AgustaWestland) — the arms-export-bribery pattern is structural, not a one-off. Foreign ownership is firewalled via FOCI proxies (Leonardo DRS) rather than barred. And the same conglomerate (Leonardo) spans arms AND domestic ALPR surveillance (ELSAG). On the PQC patent thread: a real QRL/XMSS secure-comms patent links the post-quantum-ledger idea to defense secure-comms, but the headline 'Lockheed used QRL' claim is unverified and withheld pending a primary-source assignee check — the corpus's verify-before-asserting discipline applied. Overlay; excluded from the proofs.

Google Patents — US20240048369A1 'Quantum resistant ledger for secure communications' (XMSS + QRL)

edges.raw_instrument: RTX Corp is the parent of Raytheon (+ Pratt & Whitney, Collins)
$950M+ (Oct 2024): Qatar foreign bribery (FCPA) + Patriot/radar defective pricing + export-control fraud; $230.4M criminal + $36M forfeiture
Italian state owns ~30% of Leonardo via the Ministry of Economy & Finance
Italian Ministry of Economy & Finance (state organ)
Italian parent of the US-listed subsidiary
operates under a DoD FOCI proxy agreement — cleared US persons hold the voting rights
DoD mechanism mitigating foreign ownership/control/influence over classified programs
Leonardo division — automatic license-plate readers (ALPR) for US law enforcement
